title: Send An AGV To The Hospital To Drop The Tote And Reinitialize
summary: Use the AGV Go-to function as a recovery method to send an AGV to the hospital so the tote can be removed there, allowing the AGV to drop, reinitialize, and return to picking up a new tote instead of being physically moved.
when_to_use: Use when an AGV recovery situation requires sending the AGV to the hospital so its tote can be removed and the AGV can reinitialize and receive a new task. The source specifically describes this as a quick recovery approach using Go-to instead of physically moving the AGV out, after the AGV has been removed and added back in the described recovery example.
not_for: Do not use when the source conditions for Go-to are not met, such as when the AGV is still in a task.
Do not use as justification to invent additional recovery branches beyond the source-backed hospital drop-off and tote removal sequence.
Do not treat this source as approval for broad AGV removal actions on an active system beyond the specific recovery example described.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Select the AGV, use the Go-to function, confirm the hospital destination, and verify the AGV returns to normal operation as described by the source.
Ensure the tote is removed at the hospital so WCS can reinitialize the AGV.

safety_notes: The source presents this as a way to avoid physically moving or pushing the AGV out by using the Go-to function instead.
The source indicates WCS will not reinitialize the AGV until the tote is removed and added again if the AGV remains in an improper bound state.

A nearby source segment warns that removing an AGV on an active system is a high-risk action and may be restricted by credentials; this runbook does not expand AGV removal steps beyond the source example.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the AGV control interface with robot selection
Map or destination selection including hospital
Physical access or process access to remove the tote at the hospital

access_required: Access to robot or AGV selection in the control interface
Access to the hospital destination on the map or destination selector
system_preconditions: The AGV is not in a task, as described for use of the Go-to function.
The AGV has been removed and added back in the recovery example referenced by the source.
The tote can be removed at the hospital so reinitialization can occur.

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing the AGV Go-to function workflow and describing hospital recovery use.

steps.title: Remove and add the AGV back as part of the recovery example
steps.purpose: Place the AGV into the recovery state described by the source before using Go-to for hospital drop-off.
steps.instruction: Perform the AGV remove-and-add action as referenced in the source recovery example before proceeding with the hospital Go-to workflow.
steps.expected_result: The AGV has been removed and added back as described in the source example and is ready for the next recovery action.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is available for the described Go-to recovery flow.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV remains in an improper bound or unrecovered state that prevents reinitialization progress.

actions.label: Remove and add AGV back
actions.exact_action: Remove the AGV and add it back, as described in the source recovery example.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Recovery explanation tying remove-and-add of the AGV to later hospital drop-off and reinitialization.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the AGV remains in an improper bound state and does not proceed toward reinitialization.
Stop or escalate if local permissions or approved process do not allow the remove-and-add action.

source_evidence.value: The transcript also highlights a recovery scenario where a tipper or tote-related issue leaves an AGV stuck; after removing and adding the AGV back, the operator can send it to the hospital to drop the tote so WCS can reinitialize the AGV and allow it to receive a new task.
WCS will not reinitialize the AGV until the tote is removed and added again because remove-and-add can leave it still considered bound, but not properly bound.

steps.title: Use Go-to instead of physically moving the AGV
steps.purpose: Use the source-supported recovery method that avoids physically moving the AGV out.
steps.instruction: Use the Go-to function for this recovery instead of physically moving the AGV out.
steps.expected_result: The recovery proceeds through the AGV interface and map-based destination selection rather than manual physical movement.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV can be controlled through the Go-to function for repositioning.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be sent through Go-to or the operator must resort to unsupported physical movement not described by the source.

actions.label: Choose Go-to recovery method
actions.exact_action: Use the Go to function instead of physically moving the AGV out.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Go-to function description stating it saves the operator from pushing the AGV and supports recovery repositioning.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the AGV is still in a task and therefore does not meet the source condition for Go-to use.
Stop or escalate if the interface does not allow Go-to selection for the AGV.

source_evidence.value: The Go-to function is helpful because it saves the operator from pushing the AGV; an AGV not in a task can be sent to another square or map cell.
This source describes the AGV Go-to function used when an AGV is not in a task and needs to be sent to another square or map cell.

steps.title: Select the AGV and choose the hospital destination
steps.purpose: Direct the AGV to the hospital using the source-defined Go-to workflow.
steps.instruction: Select the AGV, select Go to, and choose the hospital destination on the map or destination selection used for recovery.
steps.expected_result: The hospital destination is selected for the AGV in the Go-to workflow.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct AGV is selected and the hospital destination is available for selection.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be selected, Go-to is unavailable, or the hospital destination cannot be selected.

actions.label: Choose hospital destination
actions.exact_action: Select which square to send the AGV to, using the hospital destination for this recovery.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection Go-to workflow and the recovery use of hospital as the destination.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the AGV is not idle or not eligible for Go-to.
Stop or escalate if the hospital destination cannot be identified in the available map or destination selector.

source_evidence.value: The slide OCR lists steps: select an AGV, select Go to, select which square to send the AGV to, and select Confirm.
Instead of physically moving the AGV out, it can be sent to the hospital using Go-to; once the tote is removed there, the AGV drops, reinitializes, and picks up a new tote.

steps.title: Confirm the hospital destination
steps.purpose: Commit the Go-to instruction so the AGV navigates to the hospital.
steps.instruction: Confirm the selected destination so the AGV navigates to the hospital.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is sent toward the hospital destination.
steps.healthy_condition: The Go-to request is accepted and the AGV begins navigating to the selected destination.
steps.failure_condition: The destination is not confirmed or the AGV does not begin moving toward the hospital.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Final confirm step in the Go-to workflow.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the AGV does not accept the destination or does not navigate toward the hospital.

source_evidence.value: The slide OCR lists steps: select an AGV, select Go to, select which square to send the AGV to, and select Confirm.

steps.title: Remove the tote at the hospital
steps.purpose: Allow the AGV to drop and reinitialize by removing the tote at the hospital as described by the source.
steps.instruction: At the hospital, remove the tote from the AGV so the AGV can drop and reinitialize.
steps.expected_result: The tote is removed at the hospital and the AGV is able to drop and reinitialize.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote is no longer on the AGV and the AGV proceeds into the described reinitialization behavior.
steps.failure_condition: The tote remains on the AGV or WCS does not reinitialize the AGV because the tote remains improperly bound.

actions.label: Remove tote at hospital
actions.exact_action: Remove the tote from the AGV at the hospital.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital area flow showing hospital entrance queue and drop-off context.
Recovery explanation stating the tote is removed at the hospital so the AGV can reinitialize.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the tote cannot be removed at the hospital.
Stop or escalate if WCS does not reinitialize the AGV after tote removal because the tote remains improperly bound.

source_evidence.value: Instead of physically moving the AGV out, it can be sent to the hospital using Go-to; once the tote is removed there, the AGV drops, reinitializes, and picks up a new tote.
WCS will not reinitialize the AGV until the tote is removed and added again because remove-and-add can leave it still considered bound, but not properly bound.

The transcript also highlights a recovery scenario where a tipper or tote-related issue leaves an AGV stuck; after removing and adding the AGV back, the operator can send it to the hospital to drop the tote so WCS can reinitialize the AGV and allow it to receive a new task.
The hospital is used as a home base, for reject handling when an AGV cannot tip, for initializing new AGVs, and for shutdown-state behavior when AGVs are removed or added.

steps.title: Verify the AGV reinitializes and picks up a new tote
steps.purpose: Confirm the recovery succeeded and the AGV returned to normal task flow.
steps.instruction: Verify that the AGV comes back and picks up a new tote after the hospital tote removal and reinitialization sequence.
steps.expected_result: The AGV reinitializes and receives a new task or tote as described by the source.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV comes back and picks up a new tote.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV does not reinitialize, does not receive a new task, or remains in an improper bound state.

actions.label: Verify AGV recovery outcome
actions.exact_action: Verify that the AGV comes back and picks up a new tote.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Recovery outcome stating the AGV reinitializes and picks up a new tote.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the AGV does not pick up a new tote after hospital drop-off and tote removal.
Stop or escalate if WCS still does not reinitialize the AGV.

source_evidence.value: Instead of physically moving the AGV out, it can be sent to the hospital using Go-to; once the tote is removed there, the AGV drops, reinitializes, and picks up a new tote.

The transcript also highlights a recovery scenario where a tipper or tote-related issue leaves an AGV stuck; after removing and adding the AGV back, the operator can send it to the hospital to drop the tote so WCS can reinitialize the AGV and allow it to receive a new task.

success_criteria: The AGV is sent to the hospital using Go-to.
The tote is removed at the hospital.
The AGV comes back and picks up a new tote or receives a new task as described by the source.
healthy_conditions: The AGV is not in a task and can be sent using Go-to.
The hospital destination can be selected and confirmed.
The tote is removed at the hospital.
WCS reinitializes the AGV after tote removal.
The AGV returns to normal flow and picks up a new tote.
failure_conditions: The AGV is still in a task and cannot be sent using Go-to.
The AGV cannot be selected or the Go-to function is unavailable.
The hospital destination cannot be selected or confirmed.
The tote is not removed at the hospital.
WCS does not reinitialize the AGV because the tote remains improperly bound.
The AGV does not come back or does not pick up a new tote.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the AGV remains in an improper bound state after remove-and-add and hospital tote removal.
Escalate if the tote cannot be removed at the hospital.
Escalate if WCS does not reinitialize the AGV after the tote is removed.
Escalate if permissions or approved process do not allow the required AGV recovery actions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary Go-to workflow and hospital recovery explanation.

missing_details: The source does not provide the exact remove-and-add AGV interface steps within this packet.
The source does not provide a precise hospital map coordinate, square code, or destination code.
The source does not provide detailed tote removal handling instructions beyond removing the tote at the hospital.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this recovery.
The source does not explicitly define escalation ownership beyond indicating when reinitialization does not occur.
